Protein names: RNA/DNA methyltransferase Rv2966c (16S rRNA m2G966 methyltransferase) (5-methylcytosine-specific DNA methyltransferase) (EC 2.1.1.-) (Ribosomal RNA small subunit methyltransferase D) (EC 2.1.1.171) (RsmD-like methyltransferase)
Domain [CC]: DOMAIN: Forms a two-domain structure with a short hairpin domain at the N-terminus and a large C-terminal domain with the S-adenosylmethionine-MT fold (PubMed:21474448). The N-terminal domain helps target recognition and is important for binding RNA and DNA substrates (PubMed:21474448). The C-terminal residues dictate the nuclear localization (PubMed:25824946). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:21474448, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25824946}.
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase superfamily. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=guanosine(966) in 16S rRNA + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = N(2)-methylguanosine(966) in 16S rRNA + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:23548, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10211, Rhea:RHEA-COMP:10212, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:74269, ChEBI:CHEBI:74481; EC=2.1.1.171; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:21474448}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:23549; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:21474448};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: SAM-dependent methyltransferase that binds both to RNA and DNA (PubMed:21474448). Specifically methylates the guanine in position 966 of 16S rRNA in the 30S particle (PubMed:21474448). In addition, can methylate and modulate host cellular DNA within an infected mammalian cell, altering the host epigenetic machinery (PubMed:25824946). Binds to specific host DNA sequences and methylates cytosines predominantly in a non-CpG context (PubMed:25824946). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:21474448, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25824946}.
